name: Mediterranean Shrimp Flatbread
description: The Mediterranean Dish's shrimp flatbread 'pizza' — crisp flatbread topped with shrimp, sun-dried and grape tomatoes, shallot, bell pepper, oregano and parsley, finished with a light feta and lemon. Light on cheese; scaled to serve 4.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb cooked peeled shrimp (thawed, patted dry)
recipeInstructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. If you want a sturdier base, crisp the bare flatbreads on a sheet pan for 3–4 minutes first.
Pat the cooked shrimp dry. In a bowl, toss the shrimp, sun-dried tomatoes, shallot, bell pepper, grape tomatoes, oregano, and a drizzle of olive oil with a pinch of salt.
Brush the flatbreads lightly with olive oil. Scatter the shrimp-vegetable mixture evenly over the top, then add a light crumble of feta (skip the parmesan to keep it low-cheese).
Bake 10–12 minutes, until the flatbread is crisp at the edges and everything is warmed through. Don't overbake — the shrimp are already cooked.
Finish with fresh parsley, a squeeze of lemon, red pepper flakes if using, and a drizzle of good olive oil. Cut and serve warm.
author: The Mediterranean Dish

ai.cliffNotes: A fast shrimp flatbread 'pizza' — crisp flatbread under shrimp, sun-dried + grape tomatoes, shallot, pepper and herbs, with just a light feta. Naturally low-cheese and protein-forward, perfect alongside pizza night.
keyTips: Pat the shrimp dry and use already-cooked shrimp added straight onto the bread — they only need to warm through. Overbaking turns them rubbery, so pull as soon as the flatbread crisps.
Pre-crisp the bare flatbread for a few minutes before topping if you want a sturdier base under the juicy tomatoes.
Going light on feta (and skipping the parmesan) is no loss here — sun-dried tomatoes, shallot, and oregano bring plenty of savory depth, which is exactly why this works as a low-cheese pizza.
Finish AFTER baking with fresh parsley, a squeeze of lemon, and good olive oil to keep it bright and Mediterranean.
